pages.title: Overview
pages.body:
Three meshes each use a different animation curve:

· Red sphere — Math.sin(elapsed): smooth oscillation
· Green box — (elapsed % 1) * 2 - 1: linear sawtooth
· Yellow torus — easeInOut(t): smooth start and end

The easeInOut function at the top of the file is the mathematical core of the third pattern.

pages.title: DOM Label Helpers
pages.body:
makeLabelEl() and placeLabel() together create canvas-following labels.

placeLabel() projects a mesh's 3D position to screen space using Three.js's project() method, then converts the -1..1 NDC range to pixel coordinates. This is the standard pattern for any element that needs to follow a 3D object.

pages.title: setup() — creating objects
pages.body:
Three objects are created from a descriptor array. Each has a shape, color, and x-position. The geometry is chosen by the shape string, materials share the pattern of setting emissive to a dim version of the base color.

Dom labels are created and appended here, stored alongside their mesh for update() to reposition.

pages.title: update() — three animation patterns
pages.body:
Four values are computed each frame from ctx.elapsed:

· sv = Math.sin(t) — oscillates -1..1 smoothly
· lv = (t % 1) * 2 - 1 — linear ramp that resets every second
· et = (t % 2) / 2 — 0..1 input for easeInOut
· ev = easeInOut(et) * 2 - 1 — smooth start/end output

All three meshes rotate at the same speed (0.5 * dt) so differences come purely from the Y-position curves.

pages.title: teardown()
pages.body: DOM elements created in setup() must be removed manually. Three.js meshes added with ctx.add() are cleaned up by the Artlab runtime — you only need to handle DOM elements you created yourself.
